title: Gold PPO 500 plan summary
text: Gold PPO 500 is a PPO plan in the BlueCare Choice PPO network. Individual deductible is $1500. Individual out-of-pocket maximum is $6500. In-network coinsurance is 20 percent after deductible when deductible applies. Out-of-network coinsurance is 40 percent after deductible and balance billing may apply. PCP is not required and specialist referral is not required.

title: Silver HMO 1000 plan summary
text: Silver HMO 1000 is an HMO plan in the CareFirst HMO Network. Individual deductible is $1000. Individual out-of-pocket maximum is $5000. In-network coinsurance is 10 percent after deductible when deductible applies. PCP is required. Specialist referral is required. Out-of-network care is generally not covered except for emergencies.

title: Bronze HDHP 3000 plan summary
text: Bronze HDHP 3000 is a high deductible health plan in the ValueCare PPO network. Individual deductible is $3000. Individual out-of-pocket maximum is $7000. Deductible applies to most services before the plan pays coinsurance. In-network coinsurance is 20 percent after deductible.

title: Gold PPO primary care benefit
text: For Gold PPO 500, an in-network primary care visit has a $25 copay. The deductible does not apply. Prior authorization is not required.

title: Gold PPO specialist benefit
text: For Gold PPO 500, an in-network specialist visit has a $45 copay. The deductible does not apply. Referral is not required for PPO specialist visits.

title: Gold PPO urgent care benefit
text: For Gold PPO 500, in-network urgent care has a $60 copay. The deductible does not apply. Urgent care is usually less expensive than emergency room care.

title: Gold PPO MRI imaging benefit
text: For Gold PPO 500, MRI imaging is covered at 20 percent coinsurance after deductible. Prior authorization is required for advanced imaging such as MRI.

title: Silver HMO specialist benefit
text: For Silver HMO 1000, an in-network specialist visit has a $35 copay. The deductible does not apply. A PCP referral is required before specialist care.

title: Silver HMO out-of-network rules
text: Silver HMO 1000 generally does not cover out-of-network care except emergency services. Members should use CareFirst HMO Network providers and coordinate through the assigned PCP.

title: MRI claim denial example for M001
text: Claim CLM1002 for member M001 was for MRI imaging at Westside Imaging Center on 2026-04-09. Amount billed was $1450. Status is denied. Denial reason: prior authorization was not obtained. Next step: ask the provider to submit medical records or contact Member Services for an appeal.

title: Specialist referral claim example for M002
text: Claim CLM1003 for member M002 was for a specialist visit with Dr. Sara Williams on 2026-05-01. Status is pending referral review. Reason: referral missing from PCP. Next step: contact the PCP to submit a referral.

title: Phone number update workflow
text: For security, phone number updates must be completed through the secure profile update form or Member Services verification. The assistant can explain the steps but should not directly update the phone number in chat.

title: Claim denial reasons
text: Claims can be denied for reasons such as missing prior authorization, inactive coverage, missing referral, non-covered service, duplicate billing, or incomplete provider documentation. Specific claim disputes should be reviewed by Member Services.

title: Medical emergency guidance
text: If the member may be having a medical emergency, the assistant must tell them to call 911 immediately or go to the nearest emergency room. The assistant must not diagnose symptoms or delay emergency care.

title: Changing plans during open enrollment or special enrollment
text: Members can usually change plans during open enrollment. Outside open enrollment, a qualifying life event is required, such as marriage, birth, adoption, or loss of other coverage. The member should submit the plan change or special enrollment form for verification.

title: Adding a dependent
text: Eligible dependents may include a spouse, domestic partner where allowed, children, adopted children, and certain legal dependents. A member may add a dependent during open enrollment or after a qualifying life event. The member should submit the dependent verification form and the plan will provide an update after review.

title: Dependent access and family permissions
text: Access to dependent information depends on relationship, dependent age, consent, and privacy rules. Adult dependents generally need to grant permission. Members can manage family access in the secure portal.

title: Deductible definition
text: Deductible means the amount a member pays before the plan begins paying for services that apply to the deductible.

title: Copay definition
text: Copay means a fixed amount the member pays for a covered service, such as a primary care visit, specialist visit, or urgent care.

title: Coinsurance definition
text: Coinsurance means the percentage of covered costs the member pays after meeting any applicable deductible.

title: Group ID definition
text: Group ID is the identifier for the employer or group plan associated with the member's health coverage. It can appear on the member ID card.

title: MRI prior authorization rule
text: MRI imaging usually requires prior authorization. PPO members typically need prior authorization for advanced imaging. HMO members may need both PCP referral and prior authorization.
